{"id":"Synaxis-95je","title":"Merge InferenceGateway Code","description":"
## Context
Existing InferenceGateway/ code contains valuable functionality that should be migrated into the new Inference bounded context. This preserves existing investments while aligning with the new architecture.

## Technical Details
Migrate existing InferenceGateway/ code with:

**Discovery Phase:**
1. Analyze InferenceGateway/ directory structure
2. Identify all components and functionality
3. Map components to new architecture layers
4. Identify reusable code vs. code to refactor

**Migration Strategy:**

1. **Domain Layer:**
   - Extract domain models
   - Extract value objects
   - Extract domain events
   - Refactor to follow DDD patterns
   - Add missing invariants

2. **Application Layer:**
   - Extract commands and queries
   - Extract handlers
   - Refactor to use new domain model
   - Add validation

3. **Infrastructure Layer:**
   - Extract provider clients
   - Extract repository implementations
   - Refactor to use new abstractions
   - Add Cosmos DB event store

4. **API Layer:**
   - Extract controllers
   - Extract request/response models
   - Refactor to use new application services
   - Add streaming support

**Functionality to Preserve:**
- All existing provider integrations
- All existing routing logic
- All existing templates
- All existing user preferences
- All existing model configs
- All existing API endpoints

**Data Migration:**
- Migrate existing templates to new ChatTemplate aggregate
- Migrate existing preferences to new UserChatPreferences aggregate
- Migrate existing model configs to new ModelConfig aggregate
- Preserve all data relationships

**Testing:**
- Verify all migrated functionality works
- Compare old vs. new behavior
- Ensure no regressions
- Update integration tests

## Guardrails
- Preserve all existing functionality
- Don't break existing integrations
- Maintain backward compatibility where possible
- Test thoroughly before removing old code
- Use file-scoped namespaces
- Include proper XML documentation

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] InferenceGateway/ code analyzed
- [ ] Migration plan documented
- [ ] Domain layer migrated
- [ ] Application layer migrated
- [ ] Infrastructure layer migrated
- [ ] API layer migrated
- [ ] Data migrated
- [ ] All functionality preserved
- [ ] No regressions
- [ ] Old code removed
- [ ] Integration tests updated
- [ ] Code follows solution conventions
- [ ] Build passes with zero warnings

## Dos and Don'ts
**DO:**
- Preserve all functionality
- Test thoroughly
- Document migration
- Maintain compatibility
- Write comprehensive tests

**DON'T:**
- Lose existing functionality
- Skip testing
- Break integrations
- Use NoWarn suppressions

## Must/Should/Must Not
- MUST preserve all functionality
- MUST test thoroughly
- MUST document migration
- MUST have tests
- MUST NOT lose functionality
- SHOULD maintain compatibility
- SHOULD update integration tests
","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"rudimar@outlook.com","created_at":"2026-02-15T20:09:22Z","created_by":"Rudimar Ronsoni","updated_at":"2026-02-15T20:09:22Z","work_type":"mutex"}

Inference.Infrastructure project created successfully.

## Summary
- Project created at src/Inference/Inference.Infrastructure
- Build succeeds with 0 warnings, 0 errors

## Files Created
- Inference.Infrastructure.csproj - Project file with EF Core, Cosmos DB, Azure.Identity, Polly packages
- GlobalUsings.cs - Global using directives
- DependencyInjection.cs - DI registration for persistence
- Persistence/InferenceDbContext.cs - EF Core DbContext for Cosmos DB/SQL Server
- Persistence/Entities/InferenceRequestEntity.cs - Entity for inference requests
- Persistence/Entities/ModelConfigEntity.cs - Entity for model configurations
- Persistence/Interfaces/IInferenceRepository.cs - Repository interface
- Persistence/Repositories/InferenceRepository.cs - Repository implementation

## Architecture
- Clean separation between domain entities and persistence entities
- Repository pattern with EF Core
- Supports both Cosmos DB (production) and SQL Server (development)
- XML documentation on all public APIs

Completed: 11 event handlers, cache invalidation, message bus integration, 40 unit tests passing

{"id":"Synaxis-pmzc","title":"Synaxis-ary9: Improve test reliability verification and CI","description":"Improve the reliability of the test suite and CI pipeline.

## Work Required:

### 1. Test Stability Improvements:
- Add retry policies for flaky tests (max 3 retries)
- Identify and fix async timing issues
- Replace Task.Delay with proper synchronization primitives
- Ensure proper test isolation (no shared state)

### 2. CI/CD Pipeline Enhancements:
- Add test result reporting (TRX format)
- Configure test timeout thresholds (max 5 min per test)
- Add flaky test detection and reporting
- Parallel test execution configuration
- Test coverage reporting with thresholds

### 3. Test Configuration:
- Update xunit.runner.json with proper settings
- Add dotnet test flags for CI reliability
- Configure test categorization (Unit, Integration, E2E)
- Add test logging verbosity settings

### 4. Docker Integration:
- Ensure Testcontainers cleanup properly
- Add health checks for container dependencies
- Configure container startup timeouts","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"rudimar@outlook.com","created_at":"2026-03-04T10:05:54Z","created_by":"Rudimar Ronsoni","updated_at":"2026-03-04T10:05:54Z"}
